subject: Just now, President Trump keeps another promise on trade
body:
| |
---
The White House • September 24, 2018
## Just now, President Trump keeps another promise on trade
##
##
**"For decades politicians** have talked about fixing broken trade deals only to do absolutely nothing about them,” President Donald J. Trump said this afternoon from New York. “My Administration is the first to actually keep our promise and deliver.” **In New York City for the 73rd session** of the United Nations General Assembly this week, President Trump met with South Korean President Moon Jae-in today to sign a new trade agreement that protects American workers and businesses. The revised deal will increase U.S. exports while preserving jobs in America’s auto industry. **Under the old U.S.–Korea Free Trade Agreement (KORUS)** signed in 2012, our trade deficit skyrocketed from 6.3 billion to $9.8 billion by 2017. Under the new arrangement, South Korea will double the annual number of American automobiles that can enter its market using U.S. safety standards. Seoul has also agreed to address issues with onerous and costly customs procedures that have hindered American exports. **The Republic of Korea is** **a key ally** and trading partner of the United States; improving U.S.–Korean trade will help to strengthen this crucial economic and national security relationship. “Our two countries have set an example of friendship and cooperation for trade that rarely you see in this age,” President Trump said. [**Watch the historic signing ceremony between the United States and South Korea**](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwOTI0Ljk1Mjk4ODkxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDkyNC45NTI5ODg5MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzkwMTA1JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&100&&&https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jCFZBuo-OtM) **Get the facts** : **[How America’s newest trade deal puts our workers first](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwOTI0Ljk1Mjk4ODkxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDkyNC45NTI5ODg5MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzkwMTA1JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&101&&&https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/president-donald-j-trump-fulfilling-promise-united-states-korea-free-trade-agreement-national-security/?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=wh?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d) **
## A drug epidemic without borders
##
**Kicking off the week** at U.N. headquarters in New York,**** President Trump addressed an issue that his Administration has worked tirelessly to address since Inauguration Day: a deadly drug epidemic that spills across national borders. **Last month, the United States announced** a “Global Call to Action on the World Drug Problem.” This morning, President Trump explained the effort to leaders from across the globe. “The call is simple: reduce drug demand; cut off the supply of illicit drugs; expand treatment; and strengthen international cooperation. If we take these steps together, we can save the lives of countless people in all corners of the world.” **The 2018 World Drug Report** highlights the scope of the problem and the need for action. As cocaine and opium production have hit record highs, global deaths caused by drug use increased by 60 percent from 2000 to 2015. This market for illicit drugs fuels organized crime, corruption, and terrorism the world over. **“The scourge of drug addiction** continues to claim too many lives in the United States and in nations around the world,” President Trump said. “Today, we commit to fighting the drug epidemic together.” [**Watch: President Trump at the Global Call to Action on the World Drug Problem**](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwOTI0Ljk1Mjk4ODkxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDkyNC45NTI5ODg5MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzkwMTA1JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&102&&&https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3Q0JYx1Qjzw) **New today** : [**White House announces new counties to receive Federal support to fight drug trafficking**](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwOTI0Ljk1Mjk4ODkxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDkyNC45NTI5ODg5MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzkwMTA1JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&103&&&https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/white-house-drug-policy-office-announces-new-counties-receive-federal-support-combating-drug-trafficking/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d)
## Photo of the Day

clean_body: | | The White House • September 24, 2018 Just now, President Trump keeps another promise on trade "For decades politicians have talked about fixing broken trade deals only to do absolutely nothing about them,” President Donald J. Trump said this afternoon from New York. “My Administration is the first to actually keep our promise and deliver.” In New York City for the 73rd session of the United Nations General Assembly this week, President Trump met with South Korean President Moon Jae-in today to sign a new trade agreement that protects American workers and businesses. The revised deal will increase U.S. exports while preserving jobs in America’s auto industry. Under the old U.S.–Korea Free Trade Agreement (KORUS) signed in 2012, our trade deficit skyrocketed from 6.3 billion to $9.8 billion by 2017. Under the new arrangement, South Korea will double the annual number of American automobiles that can enter its market using U.S. safety standards. Seoul has also agreed to address issues with onerous and costly customs procedures that have hindered American exports. The Republic of Korea is a key ally and trading partner of the United States; improving U.S.–Korean trade will help to strengthen this crucial economic and national security relationship. “Our two countries have set an example of friendship and cooperation for trade that rarely you see in this age,” President Trump said. Get the facts : A drug epidemic without borders Kicking off the week at U.N. headquarters in New York,** President Trump addressed an issue that his Administration has worked tirelessly to address since Inauguration Day: a deadly drug epidemic that spills across national borders. Last month, the United States announced a “Global Call to Action on the World Drug Problem.” This morning, President Trump explained the effort to leaders from across the globe. “The call is simple: reduce drug demand; cut off the supply of illicit drugs; expand treatment; and strengthen international cooperation. If we take these steps together, we can save the lives of countless people in all corners of the world.” The 2018 World Drug Report highlights the scope of the problem and the need for action. As cocaine and opium production have hit record highs, global deaths caused by drug use increased by 60 percent from 2000 to 2015. This market for illicit drugs fuels organized crime, corruption, and terrorism the world over. “The scourge of drug addiction continues to claim too many lives in the United States and in nations around the world,” President Trump said. “Today, we commit to fighting the drug epidemic together.” New today : Photo of the Day Official White House Photo by Joyce N. Boghosian President Donald J. Trump and President of South Korea Moon Jae-in sign the United States-Korea Free Trade Agreement** | September 24, 2018 | |

name: Jeff
subject: thanks Obama
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Denham for Congress
body:
I’m not sure you’ve heard, but this month, Barack Obama and San Francisco Mayor Gavin Newsom are out campaigning for my opponent, Bay Area Josh......
Friend,

I’m not sure you’ve heard, but this month, Barack Obama and San Francisco Mayor Gavin Newsom are out campaigning for my opponent, Bay Area Josh Harder.

Harder may think having ultra-liberals Obama and Newsom on the trail with him is a good idea, but I would caution him to think again.

Does he even remember the Obama economy? Valley families do. We faced terrible unemployment numbers and our business owners were crushed under regulations supported by liberals like President Obama and our extreme, leftist California legislature.

Those hard times are still on the minds of many Americans, and by bringing him on board, Josh Harder has proven that he is out of touch with Valley voters.

And Gavin Newsom? He’s nothing more than the posterchild for Nancy Pelosi’s extreme liberal agenda.
[REJECT Obama and Harder](http://links.jeffdenham.com/u/click?_t=ae4e127997b64fdfa10e37f90fe2a78d&_m=bda821e5cd6f44388b752195faf9e0c0&_e=iZBVLO5U89pKBRDUaGDcxkw_GOceoAiQhqV_4Zgdg1BhNercgXzujHA1jfvTd0SOJVqBWgdSDj6IMjl0q7eDv8tO5MibEgq3PpVoxNjZn8k47lauOb2LISaYWTJdL0jPNGfearKrtJtnFd7UxdEpF1y-LuRBh1W4jGpqOr9ZLkxUDyzi52nCalnWM8r9CIej0Vq7hHeuiIYar-truMXDZRe2VsTrxPRuQDZkudPDa9QLJ6VHDmsi0axtl2wlMhmnkGKR0NYk1LG1yEAKvFIqNujCMFDLQyYf_Dt1wtqFvWiQTHzhiz4aeOkstx9fcc0plnD2koiIsR7tmzyX4MN7-yT1kiRxZllCGfm8RC6H_Wu4qskG_w-h9tzBBfahy_JmCsPVHQ22t26vIra5NYH01PhAfq4ZHgKBbtSJJGHlv3o%3D)
---

Josh Harder needs to hear it loud and clear: we need lower taxes, smaller government, and safer communities. Standing with Obama and Newsom means standing against all of these. It means standing against _the Valley._

Let’s send a message to our Bay Area friend Josh. [**Liberal values are not our values, and we won’t stand for them in the Valley.**](http://links.jeffdenham.com/u/click?_t=ae4e127997b64fdfa10e37f90fe2a78d&_m=bda821e5cd6f44388b752195faf9e0c0&_e=iZBVLO5U89pKBRDUaGDcxkw_GOceoAiQhqV_4Zgdg1BhNercgXzujHA1jfvTd0SOJVqBWgdSDj6IMjl0q7eDv8tO5MibEgq3PpVoxNjZn8k47lauOb2LISaYWTJdL0jPNGfearKrtJtnFd7UxdEpF1y-LuRBh1W4jGpqOr9ZLkxUDyzi52nCalnWM8r9CIejFEkqPkxzaDApWFepvY_qD-iZt5ETPHa51rZ1Jb9PI0I4mkIrj2d6M8tPesM6MDnewrRnEfIQSD1vD-tRec-ij614rFS6757UDiNxXHqpmr7xM31PtfmrhO3J-beb_RDBv8CDPiydA61ir8IT1sSyiyrSyDoLLbVL5b_u0f3Q9q5ti5rm_bEv830hzv5Fj0rY83z5NpWKJPN6434kdDIYzBIrytYHTSQ_wGt7D56xcSw%3D)

I’m standing up for our principles and working every day to solve the many problems created by the failed leadership of the past. It’s time we move on from Obama and Newsom and continue to move our country in the right direction.

Josh Harder will take us backwards.

Jeff

Paid for by Denham for Congress

body:
ActBlue is an online platform many Democrats use to facilitate fundraising. They released a report last week detailing the grassroots money their platform processed in August, and I'm proud to report that Team Spanberger ranked among the top 10 Democratic congressional campaigns nationwide in grassroots fundraising.
| | Peter, I'm Abigail's Finance Director, and my job is to make sure we have the resources to share Abigail's story and ideas with every voter in VA-7. I'm reaching out today to share some exciting news about our efforts. ActBlue is an online platform many Democrats use to facilitate fundraising. They released a report last week detailing the grassroots money their platform processed in August, and **I 'm proud to report that Team Spanberger ranked among the _top 10 Democratic congressional campaigns nationwide_ in grassroots fundraising.** So, Peter, when we say that this community of supporters powers our campaign -- we truly mean it. [**Can you make a donation today to continue our efforts, so we can have a blockbuster September, too?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/QuO9Leb8vwaktB2pkIJjzjNO0Ca1btwgQvO0ZSXfW1s=/ZzuQial6EMODMYWeZpRy4Sxvz4WeLnbhOON3x-ELbyI=?refcode=em_fr_2018.09.24_b1_ec_all&amount=15&amounts=15,25,50,100,250,500,1000) One of the many reasons I'm proud to work for Abigail is that she is running a campaign built on grassroots support, and these incredible fundraising numbers prove it. Your [**generous donations**](https://act.myngp.com/el/QuO9Leb8vwaktB2pkIJjzjNO0Ca1btwgQvO0ZSXfW1s=/ZzuQial6EMODMYWeZpRy4Sxvz4WeLnbhOON3x-ELbyI=?refcode=em_fr_2018.09.24_b1_ec_all&amount=15&amounts=15,25,50,100,250,500,1000) throughout this campaign have made it possible for us to turn this historically Republican district into a toss-up race, all without taking corporate PAC money. We have 43 days left, and we are up against the continued attacks ads our opponent and Republican Super PACs are running. [**Our last end-of-quarter deadline of the campaign is coming up on Sunday, so you can help us end it strong with a donation today.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/QuO9Leb8vwaktB2pkIJjzjNO0Ca1btwgQvO0ZSXfW1s=/ZzuQial6EMODMYWeZpRy4Sxvz4WeLnbhOON3x-ELbyI=?refcode=em_fr_2018.09.24_b1_ec_all&amount=15&amounts=15,25,50,100,250,500,1000) Thanks, Emma Carl _Finance Director, Spanberger for Congress_ [](https://act.myngp.com/el/QuO9Leb8vwaktB2pkIJjzjNO0Ca1btwgQvO0ZSXfW1s=/ZzuQial6EMODMYWeZpRy4Sxvz4WeLnbhOON3x-ELbyI=?refcode=em_fr_2018.09.24_b1_ec_all&amount=15&amounts=15,25,50,100,250,500,1000) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/QuO9Leb8vwaktB2pkIJjzjNO0Ca1btwgQvO0ZSXfW1s=/ZzuQial6EMODMYWeZpRy4Sxvz4WeLnbhOON3x-ELbyI=?refcode=footer&amount=15&amounts=15,25,50,100,250,500,1000) _Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat and former CIA officer running for Congress against Dave Brat in Virginia 's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. name: Tim Kaine
subject: It's not enough to win
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
In two days I will be debating Corey Stewart on stage. And it'll be more than a debate about policy in Virginia. It'll be about who we are, and who we aren't. -----------------------------------------
| | \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Derek,

I know I emailed you yesterday, but I wanted to reach out again. [In two days](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f11bb/3e0906/7062d27d/3e36602b/1929317742/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-q32018-september-eoq?refcode=20180924_2_FR_TK_EOQ-TK&amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+In+two+days&utm_campaign=20180924_2_FR_TK_EOQ-TK&source=20180924_2_FR_TK_EOQ-TK") I will be debating Corey Stewart on stage. And it'll be more than a debate about policy in Virginia. It'll be about who we are, and who we aren't. It'll be about why we cannot diminish the gravity of what is happening in this country right now.

It's not enough to just win a debate or hit a goal. We need to do something big in November. We need to make sure Democratic House candidates in Virginia win in districts no one thought would be competitive. We need to be decisive in what we say to the world on Election Day. Because the world will be listening.

**If you can, Derek, please chip in online to help me get through this week of fundraising for myself and the rest of our strong Virginia Democrats:[https://secure.timkaine.com/Final-Quarterly-Public-Deadline](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f11bb/3e0906/7062d27d/3e36602a/1929317742/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-q32018-september-eoq?refcode=20180924_2_FR_TK_EOQ-TK&amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=2+-+httpssecuretimkainecomFinalQuarterlyPubl&utm_campaign=20180924_2_FR_TK_EOQ-TK&source=20180924_2_FR_TK_EOQ-TK")**

clean_body: In two days I will be debating Corey Stewart on stage. And it'll be more than a debate about policy in Virginia. It'll be about who we are, and who we aren't. ----------------------------------------- | | \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Derek, I know I emailed you yesterday, but I wanted to reach out again. "" I will be debating Corey Stewart on stage. And it'll be more than a debate about policy in Virginia. It'll be about who we are, and who we aren't. It'll be about why we cannot diminish the gravity of what is happening in this country right now. It's not enough to just win a debate or hit a goal. We need to do something big in November. We need to make sure Democratic House candidates in Virginia win in districts no one thought would be competitive. We need to be decisive in what we say to the world on Election Day. Because the world will be listening. If you can, Derek, please chip in online to help me get through this week of fundraising for myself and the rest of our strong Virginia Democrats: "" Thanks, Tim | "" * | "" "" ""

name: Team Gillum
subject: Down to the wire:
body:
\--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb36fb/9e00690/5dde5f57/24010c27/3697344695/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em18_180924_EOQ1_fnd_gq_ful__TG_ettx&source=em18_180924_EOQ1_fnd_gq_ful__TG_ettx")
Friends,

Our campaign continues to gain momentum and earn the support of folks who are ready for Mayor Andrew Gillum and Chris King's bold, progressive plans to move our state forward.

But that means Republican mega-donors and big money special interests are paying attention to the strength of this campaign too, and right now we're being attacked on TV. This election is a huge opportunity for Democrats to take back the Governor's mansion, but we're going to need your help to execute our get-out-the-vote efforts.

This Sunday is the final end-of-quarter deadline before the general election, and every in-state donation under $250 will be MATCHED.

**[We need this grassroots movement to raise another $250,000 to stay on track. Will you chip in $5, or whatever you can afford, now to help us bring it home in November?](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb36fb/9e00690/5dde5f57/24010c3f/3697344695/VEsP/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-eoq?gpsid=8_55_3831&refcode=em18_180924_EOQ1_fnd_gq_ful__TG_ettx&utm_campaign=em18_180924_EOQ1_fnd_gq_ful__TG_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=agil&firstname=&lastname=&zip=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&recur_weekly=yes&utm_content=9+-+We+need+this+grassroots+movement+to+rais")**

Everyday people have struggled to get ahead for the last two decades under Republican control. And, instead of introducing real plans to help Floridians, Ron DeSantis has spent his time using divisive, gutter politics-style attacks to tear down our campaign.

Mayor Gillum and Chris will reverse the damage caused by Rick Scott and rebuild our state and economy into one that works for every Floridian. So, if we don't want another four years of a failed administration, we need to get out the vote and reach every voter in Florida over the next few weeks.

**[The only way we can do that is by reaching our $250,000 grassroots fundraising goal. Donate $5 to have your grassroots donation doubled and help Mayor Gillum and Chris King hit our FINAL end-of-quarter deadline.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb36fb/9e00690/5dde5f57/24010c3e/3697344695/VEsHBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-eoq?gpsid=8_55_3831&refcode=em18_180924_EOQ1_fnd_gq_ful__TG_ettx&utm_campaign=em18_180924_EOQ1_fnd_gq_ful__TG_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=agil&utm_content=10+-+The+only+way+we+can+do+that+is+by+reachi")**

Bring it home,

Team Gillum

[DOUBLE MY DONATION](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb36fb/9e00690/5dde5f57/24010c31/3697344695/VEsHBA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-eoq?gpsid=8_55_3831&refcode=em18_180924_EOQ1_fnd_gq_ful__TG_ettx&utm_campaign=em18_180924_EOQ1_fnd_gq_ful__TG_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=agil&utm_content=11+-+DOUBLE+MY+DONATION")

name: Tori Taylor
subject: I have exciting news to share!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kathy Manning for Congress
body:
|
---

| | Hey -- it's Tori, Kathy's Campaign Manager. I normally send you emails about bad news. But right now, I have three pieces of exciting news to share: 1\. The _News & Observer_ shared a new poll: **_"Manning leads Budd 46 to 42."_** 2\. Nate Silver's 538 is calling this race a "Toss Up." 3\. **We are within striking distance of hitting our $65,000 FEC quarter goal. We only need another $15,000 before Sunday.[Help us close the gap.](https://manning.bsd.net/page/m/65427959/e3b7d3/5d235d57/25554c3f/900220395/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kme180924?refcode=kme180924_m&recurring=true")** Hitting this FEC goal is critical to making sure we can combat outside spending and continue spreading our message. [**Can you rush in a contribution and help us reach our last quarterly FEC goal?**](https://manning.bsd.net/page/m/65427959/e3b7d3/5d235d57/25554c3f/900220395/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kme180924?refcode=kme180924_m&recurring=true") We've worked hard, put in long days and more than a few long nights, to get where we are. But we're not done yet. Things only get harder from here. **Now that this race is anyone 's game, Rep. Ted Budd's allies have pledged to spend millions against us in the final weeks.** Rep. Budd sold out to the special interests, so you better believe they will continue to have his back. [**The deadline is closing in fast. We need to raise another $15,000 by Sunday. I know we can do it -- but we need to step up. Rush in a contribution right now.**](https://manning.bsd.net/page/m/65427959/e3b7d3/5d235d57/25554c3f/900220395/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kme180924?refcode=kme180924_m&recurring=true") Thanks. Tori Tori Taylor
Campaign Manager
Kathy Manning for Congress

name: Bob Casey
subject: This is a real problem.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bob Casey for Senate Inc
body:
[](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e6234/51dbcc2a/130554a6/3ab44ea/3928125009/VEsH/ "https://bobcasey.com/")
I'm going to give it to you straight, team: we didn't raise as much money in August as we expected, and there will be devastating consequences if we don't make up the difference by the end-of-quarter deadline on Sunday.

My team keeps sending me news clips about how Congressman Barletta is expecting a flood of outside spending any day now, how President Trump reportedly raised $1 million when he campaigned here last month and how Democrats will win or lose the Senate majority based on the strength of our campaigns in states like Pennsylvania.

**_[We need to raise another $6,300 by midnight to stay on track. Please, can you chip in $5 to help close the gap?](https://secure.bobcasey. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20180924_fr2_nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")_**

Republicans won this state in 2016, so we can't afford to be complacent. Democrats' path to the majority runs directly through Pennsylvania, and if we lose here and fail to take back the Senate, we won't be able to effectively push back against the most dangerous and divisive pieces of President Trump's agenda.

**There is too much on the line -- we simply cannot fall short. Please donate whatever you can before midnight.**

Here's the best link: **_[secure.bobcasey.com/deadline](https://secure.bobcasey. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20180924_fr2_nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")_**

Thanks,

Bob

| | Bob needs a strong team to help keep this Senate seat blue so he can continue to protect Medicare, Medicaid and Social Security, fight for working families and be a check on the Trump administration.

Will you make your first donation to help out?
[DONATE](https://secure.bobcasey. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20180924_fr2_nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")
---

Paid for by Bob Casey for Senate Inc.
---
PO Box 58746, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania 19102

Contributions or gifts to Bob Casey for Senate are not tax deductible.

Here's the best link: "" Thanks, Bob | | Bob needs a strong team to help keep this Senate seat blue so he can continue to protect Medicare, Medicaid and Social Security, fight for working families and be a check on the Trump administration. Will you make your first donation to help out? "" Paid for by Bob Casey for Senate Inc. PO Box 58746, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania 19102 Contributions or gifts to Bob Casey for Senate are not tax deductible.

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: This is a global problem
body:
[1]Bernie Sanders

Sisters and Brothers,

For the past 40 years in this country, our great middle class — once the
envy of the world — has been disappearing. All over America, people are
working two or three jobs, scared to death about the futures of their
children, while almost all new income goes to a small number of people at
the top.

But this is not a uniquely American phenomenon.

All over the world, people are seeing that same tendency. Today, in the
global economy, the top 1 percent owns more than the bottom 99 percent,
and a handful of billionaires own more than the bottom half of people
around the world — that’s 3.7 billion people.

That is the reality. People in our own country, and around the world, are
angry, and they feel that nobody is listening to their pain.

And one of the results of that reality is that in Europe, in Russia, in
the Middle East, in Asia and elsewhere we are seeing movements led by
demagogues who exploit people’s fears, prejudices and grievances to
achieve and hold on to power.

And while these regimes may differ in some respects, they share key
attributes: hostility toward democratic norms, antagonism toward a free
press, intolerance toward ethnic and religious minorities, and a belief
that government should benefit their own selfish financial interests.

These leaders are also deeply connected to a network of multi-billionaire
oligarchs, motivated by greed and power, who see the world as their
economic plaything.

This trend certainly did not begin with Trump, but there’s no question
that authoritarian leaders around the world have drawn inspiration from
the fact that the leader of the world’s oldest and most powerful democracy
seems to delight in shattering democratic norms.

Other authoritarian states are much farther along this kleptocratic
process. In Russia, it is impossible to tell where the decisions of
government end and the interests of Vladimir Putin and his circle of
oligarchs begin. They operate as one unit. Similarly, in Saudi Arabia,
there is no debate about separation because the natural resources of the
state, valued at trillions of dollars, belong to the Saudi royal family.
In Hungary, far-right authoritarian leader Viktor Orbán is openly allied
with Putin in Russia. In China, an inner circle led by Xi Jinping has
steadily consolidated power, clamping down on domestic political freedom
while it aggressively promotes a version of authoritarian capitalism
abroad.

So the question is: Where do we go from here?

To effectively oppose right-wing authoritarianism, we cannot simply go
back to the failed status quo of the last several decades. In order to
fight this trend, we need to strengthen the global coalition of
progressive democrats.

While authoritarians promote division and hatred, we will promote unity,
inclusion, and an agenda based on economic, social, racial, and
environmental justice.

Governments of the world must come together to end the absurdity of rich
and multinational corporations stashing over $21 trillion in offshore bank
accounts to avoid paying their fair share of taxes and then demanding that
their respective governments impose an austerity agenda on their working
families.

It is not acceptable that the fossil fuel industry continues to make huge
profits while their carbon emissions destroy the planet for our children
and grandchildren.

It is not acceptable that a handful of multinational media giants, owned
by a small number of billionaires, largely control the flow of information
on the planet.

It is not acceptable that trade policies that benefit large multinational
corporations and encourage a race to the bottom hurt working people
throughout the world as they are written out of public view.

It is not acceptable that, with the Cold War long behind us, countries
around the world spend over $1 trillion a year on weapons of destruction,
while millions of children die of easily treatable diseases.

In order to effectively combat the rise of the international authoritarian
axis, we need an international progressive movement that mobilizes behind
a vision of shared prosperity, security and dignity for all people and
that addresses the massive global inequality that exists, not only in
wealth but in political power as well.

Such a movement must be willing to think creatively and boldly about the
world that we would like to see.

We must take the opportunity to reconceptualize a genuinely progressive
global order based on human solidarity, an order that recognizes that
every person on this planet shares a common humanity, that we all want our
children to grow up healthy, to have a good education, have decent jobs,
drink clean water, breathe clean air and live in peace.

Our job is to reach out to those in every corner of the world who share
these values and who are fighting for a better world.

In a time of exploding wealth and technology, we have the potential to
create a decent life for all people. Our job is to build on our common
humanity and do everything that we can to oppose all of the forces,
whether unaccountable government power or unaccountable corporate power,
who try to divide us up and set us against each other.

We know that those forces work together across borders. We must do the
same.

Thank you for reading.

In solidarity,

Bernie Sanders

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

We have a modest fundraising goal to hit to support Bernie’s re-election
campaign and his work traveling the country to rally with progressive
candidates. Will you make a
$3 donation before
this week’s official FEC fundraising deadline?
[ https://act.berniesanders.com/go/2715?refcode=em180924-full&t=1001&akid=159%2E150750%2Eop2z2U ]DONATE

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.berniesanders.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=159%2E150750%2Eop2z2U

name: =?utf-8?Q?Ian=20Todd?=
subject: Root beer floats and Pizza! Help us GOTV with Tarryl Clark!
body:
** Float to the Vote Oct 4th!
------------------------------------------------------------
Join us at the Saint Cloud DFL office next Thursday for some free root beer floats, pizza, and volunteering. We will be joined by Ian Todd & Tarryl Clark as we knock on doors, make phone calls and enjoy the company of friends.

The plan is for everyone to go out and early vote, then meet up at the DFL office and hear the candidates say a few words as we enjoy the food and start volunteering.

We will be in the office from 3pm until 7pm so feel free to join us at any time.

Please RSVP on Facebook here: https://www.facebook.com/events/301604740623611/?utm_source=Newsletter&utm_campaign=0cfa0e5e84-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_09_24_07_18&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_28710cd7a6-0cfa0e5e84-9291445

The address is
1727 West St. Germain Street, St. Cloud, MN 56303

Donate (https://secure.actblue.com/donate/iantodd?refcode=sep24&amount=50&utm_source=Newsletter&utm_campaign=0cfa0e5e84-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_09_24_07_18&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_28710cd7a6-0cfa0e5e84-9291445)
